{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"class ResidualBlock(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels: int, out_channels: int, stride: int = 1, dropout: float = 0.0, use_bn: bool = True):
        super().__init__()
        bias = not use_bn
        layers1: list[nn.Module] = [
            nn.Conv2d(in_channels, out_channels, 3, stride=stride, padding=1, bias=bias)
        ]
        layers2: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(out_channels, out_channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if use_bn:
            layers1.insert(1, nn.BatchNorm2d(out_channels))
            layers2.insert(1, nn.BatchNorm2d(out_channels))
        self.conv1 = nn.Sequential(*layers1)
        self.conv2 = nn.Sequential(*layers2)
        if stride != 1 or in_channels != out_channels:
            shortcut_layers: list[nn.Module] = [
                nn.Conv2d(in_channels, out_channels, 1, stride=stride, bias=bias)
            ]
            if use_bn:
                shortcut_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(out_channels))
            self.shortcut = nn.Sequential(*shortcut_layers)
        else:
            self.shortcut = nn.Identity()
        self.activation = nn.ReLU(inplace=True)
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout2d(dropout) if dropout > 0 else nn.Identity()

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        residual = self.shortcut(x)
        out = self.activation(self.conv1(x))
        out = self.dropout(out)
        out = self.conv2(out)
        out = self.activation(out + residual)
        return out","old":"class ResidualBlock(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, channels: int, dropout: float = 0.0, use_bn: bool = True):
        super().__init__()
        bias = not use_bn
        layers1: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        layers2: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if use_bn:
            layers1.insert(1, nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
            layers2.insert(1, nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        self.conv1 = nn.Sequential(*layers1)
        self.conv2 = nn.Sequential(*layers2)
        self.activation = nn.ReLU(inplace=True)
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout2d(dropout) if dropout > 0 else nn.Identity()

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        residual = x
        out = self.activation(self.conv1(x))
        out = self.dropout(out)
        out = self.conv2(out)
        out = self.activation(out + residual)
        return out","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        c1 = BASE_CHANNELS
        c2 = BASE_CHANNELS * CHANNEL_MULT
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, c1, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(c1))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            ResidualBlock(c1, c1, stride=1, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(c1, c2, stride=2, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(c2, c2, stride=1, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(c2, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.blocks(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","old":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.blocks(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Upgrade the micro residual CNN into a clearer two-stage ResNet with projection shortcuts and one stride-2 transition, improving feature hierarchy and capacity while keeping compute controlled by moving later processing to lower resolution.","secondary_modes":[]}

LR_DECAY_EPOCHS = [60, 80]","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=total_steps)","old":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = steps_per_epoch * 100
    return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=total_steps)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The current cosine schedule is effectively inactive for a 256-step run because it is stretched across 100 epochs. Switching to AdamW with a slightly higher LR and tying cosine decay to the actual verifier step budget should improve early optimization within the fixed training horizon.","secondary_modes":["indexing","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"This run is fixed at 256 optimizer steps, so increasing batch size lets the model see substantially more training examples within the same step budget. {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 3","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current network is still very small in its lower-resolution stage, where extra channels are relatively cheap. {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The model is now deeper and trains for only a short horizon, so the current weight decay may be overly restrictive relative to how much fitting still needs to happen. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The current deeper network continues to improve with only modest signs of instability, and recent tweaks have yielded small but positive gains. {"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    warmup_steps = min(max(1, steps_per_epoch // 4), max(1, total_steps // 16))","old":"    warmup_steps = min(max(1, steps_per_epoch // 2), max(1, total_steps // 8))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The 3e-3 run overshot, but the deeper model may still be spending too much of a short 256-step budget in a conservative warmup phase. {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    warmup_steps = min(max(1, steps_per_epoch // 2), max(1, total_steps // 8))","old":"    warmup_steps = min(max(1, steps_per_epoch // 4), max(1, total_steps // 16))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The shortened warmup clearly hurt, so restore the better schedule and spend the remaining budget on a very cheap capacity increase in the classifier head. {"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Restore the better classifier width, then use a slightly smaller AdamW second-moment timescale.
